leaderboard.title: Leaderboard
leaderboard.note: Assets are valued at the live market price. Cash includes funds reserved for open limit orders. Updates every 10 seconds.

aiPage.title: Connect an AI assistant
aiPage.intro1: de BereBank has a built-in MCP server (Model Context Protocol — the open standard AI assistants use to talk to external services). Connect ChatGPT or Claude to your account and you can ask things like "how is my portfolio doing?", "what did Bitcoin do in the last 24 hours?" or "show my best trades this month" — the assistant looks up live answers directly from de BereBank.
aiPage.intro2: You sign in with your normal BereBank email and password when connecting; there are no API keys to manage. Access uses your own account, so the assistant sees exactly what you see.
aiPage.agentsGuideIntro: AGENTS.md explains the purpose of the BereBank simulation, how the competition works, every MCP tool, and a suggested workflow for developing an investment strategy. Give it to your assistant so it understands the game before you start trading.
aiPage.agentsGuideStep1: Open the link above (or copy the URL) and skim the guide yourself — it describes the leaderboard goal and what assistants can do via MCP.
aiPage.agentsGuideStep2: In Cursor: add AGENTS.md as project context (@AGENTS.md in chat, or reference it in your rules) before asking the agent to help with strategy or trades.
aiPage.agentsGuideStep3: In Claude or ChatGPT: paste the AGENTS.md URL into the conversation, upload the file, or ask the assistant to read it — then connect the MCP server below.
aiPage.agentsGuideStep4: Start with something like: "Read AGENTS.md, then check my portfolio and suggest a strategy for the competition." Enable MCP trading in your profile only when you want the assistant to place orders.
aiPage.whatRead: Always: read market prices, charts, your portfolio, orders and trade history.
aiPage.whatTrade: Only if you allow it: place and cancel orders. This is controlled by the "Allow trading via MCP" switch in the MCP access section of your profile — it is off by default and you can turn it off again at any time, effective immediately.
aiPage.claudeIntro: Works on claude.ai and in the Claude desktop app (custom connectors are available on all plans; free accounts are limited to one).
aiPage.claudeStep1: In Claude, go to Settings → Connectors (on claude.ai: Customize → Connectors).
aiPage.claudeStep3: Paste the MCP server URL shown above and click Add. You can leave the advanced OAuth fields empty.
aiPage.claudeStep4: Click Connect: a BereBank login page opens. Sign in with your BereBank email and password and click "Allow access".
aiPage.claudeStep5: In a conversation, enable the connector via the tools (+) menu, then just ask about markets or your portfolio.
aiPage.chatgptIntro: Custom MCP connectors in ChatGPT require a paid plan and Developer mode.
aiPage.chatgptStep1: In ChatGPT, go to Settings → Apps & Connectors → Advanced settings and enable "Developer mode".
aiPage.chatgptStep2: Go back to Apps & Connectors and click "Create" (add custom connector).
aiPage.chatgptStep3: Enter a name (e.g. "de BereBank"), paste the MCP server URL shown above and choose OAuth as authentication.
aiPage.chatgptStep4: Accept the notice about custom connectors and save.
aiPage.chatgptStep5: A BereBank login page opens. Sign in with your BereBank email and password and click "Allow access".
aiPage.chatgptStep6: In a conversation, enable the connector via the tools menu, then ask away.
aiPage.example1: "How is my portfolio doing? What is my total account value?"
aiPage.example2: "Which EUR markets moved the most in the last 24 hours?"
aiPage.example3: "Show my trade history with profit/loss. Which trade was my best?"
aiPage.example4: "Buy EUR 50 of Bitcoin." (only works if you allowed trading via MCP)
aiPage.disclaimer: Remember: de BereBank is a simulation — trades use paper money, but the market data is real. If you allow trading via MCP, an AI assistant can really place orders on your (paper) account, so keep the switch off unless you want that.

profile.title: Your profile
profile.languageNote: Applied automatically every time you log in.

mcp.title: MCP access
mcp.intro: Connect an AI assistant (Claude, Cursor, …) to your account via the Model Context Protocol. Market and portfolio information is always readable; trading is only allowed if you enable it below.
mcp.tradingNote: When enabled, connected assistants can place and cancel orders on your behalf. Turning it off takes effect immediately.

password.title: Change password
password.tooShort: New password must be at least 6 characters

login.tagline: Simulated crypto exchange — practice trading with paper money
login.noAccount: No account? Ask your bank manager to create one.

portfolio.emptyHoldings: You don't hold any crypto yet.
portfolio.noOpenLimitOrders: No open limit or stop-loss orders

SPY.name: SPDR S&P 500 ETF
SPY.description: Tracks the S&P 500 index — 500 of the largest US companies. One of the most traded ETFs worldwide and a common benchmark for US large-cap returns.

QQQ.name: Invesco QQQ Trust
QQQ.description: Tracks the Nasdaq-100: the 100 largest non-financial companies listed on Nasdaq. Heavily weighted toward technology and growth stocks such as Apple, Microsoft, and Nvidia.

VOO.name: Vanguard S&P 500 ETF
VOO.description: A low-cost ETF that mirrors the S&P 500. Very similar to SPY but with a lower expense ratio — popular for long-term buy-and-hold strategies.

VTI.name: Vanguard Total Stock Market ETF
VTI.description: Covers virtually the entire US equity market — from large caps to small caps. Broader than the S&P 500 alone and often used as a single-fund US portfolio.

IWM.name: iShares Russell 2000 ETF
IWM.description: Tracks the Russell 2000 index of US small-cap companies. More volatile than large-cap funds but can outperform in strong economic upswings.

VEA.name: Vanguard FTSE Developed Markets ETF
VEA.description: Invests in large- and mid-cap stocks across developed markets outside the US (Europe, Japan, Canada, etc.). Adds international diversification.

VWO.name: Vanguard FTSE Emerging Markets ETF
VWO.description: Covers emerging-market countries such as China, India, and Brazil. Higher growth potential but also higher volatility and political/currency risk.

BND.name: Vanguard Total Bond Market ETF
BND.description: A broad basket of US investment-grade bonds (government and corporate). Typically less volatile than stocks and used to reduce portfolio risk.

TLT.name: iShares 20+ Year Treasury Bond ETF
TLT.description: Holds long-dated US Treasury bonds. Prices move inversely to interest rates — useful as a hedge when rates fall, but sensitive to rate hikes.

SCHD.name: Schwab US Dividend Equity ETF
SCHD.description: Focuses on high-quality US companies with a strong track record of paying dividends. Popular with income-oriented investors.

XLK.name: Technology Select Sector SPDR
XLK.description: Tracks the S&P 500 technology sector — hardware, software, semiconductors, and IT services. A pure sector bet on US tech.

VGT.name: Vanguard Information Technology ETF
VGT.description: Another broad US technology ETF, holding companies like Apple, Nvidia, and Microsoft. Slightly different index weighting than XLK.

SOXX.name: iShares Semiconductor ETF
SOXX.description: Invests in companies that design, manufacture, and distribute semiconductors. Highly cyclical and tied to the global chip industry.

IGV.name: iShares Expanded Tech-Software ETF
IGV.description: Focuses on software and IT services companies — SaaS, cloud platforms, and enterprise software. Less hardware-heavy than SOXX or XLK.

ARKK.name: ARK Innovation ETF
ARKK.description: An actively managed fund targeting disruptive innovation: AI, genomics, fintech, and next-gen internet. More volatile than passive index ETFs.

IBIT.name: iShares Bitcoin Trust
IBIT.description: A spot Bitcoin ETF that holds BTC directly. Offers regulated exchange-traded exposure to Bitcoin without managing a crypto wallet.

GLD.name: SPDR Gold Shares
GLD.description: Tracks the price of gold bullion. Often used as a hedge against inflation or market stress, with low correlation to stocks.

news.title: Latest news
news.empty: No recent news for this asset
news.source: News from RSS feeds and press releases

orderForm.syncNote: excl. fee · synced with the {{asset}} amount
orderForm.allReserved: All your {{asset}} is reserved in open orders — cancel an order to sell

stopLoss.title: Stop-loss for {{asset}}
stopLoss.description: Protect your position: when the price drops to the trigger price, the amount is sold automatically at the live bid price (taker fee). The amount is reserved while the stop-loss is active.

history.fifoNote: Profit/loss is calculated per sell using FIFO: the oldest purchases (including their fees) are matched first. "Held" is the amount-weighted age of those purchases at the moment of sale.

admin.deleteConfirm: Permanently delete {{name}} and all their trading history? This cannot be undone.
admin.credentialsNote: Public market data works without credentials. API keys are only needed for future authenticated Bitvavo features.
admin.twelvedataNote: US stocks and funds are priced via twelvedata.com. Enter your Twelve Data API key to enable the feed; prices refresh every minute and USD instruments are converted to EUR automatically.
admin.rssNote: RSS feeds are polled every hour. Articles matching an asset ticker or name are saved and shown on the trade page.
admin.rssFeedDeleteConfirm: Remove this RSS feed? Saved articles from this feed will also be deleted.
